changes.finding: CONFIRMED. backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml read via Python key extraction. Canonical keys relevant to staged PSI estate include: alt, ggt, homocysteine, urea, apob, non_hdl_cholesterol, total_cholesterol, tc_hdl_ratio, ferritin, hematocrit, mcv, rdw_cv, rbc, platelets, transferrin, iron, transferrin_saturation, lymphocytes, lymphocytes_abs, neutrophils, neutrophils_abs, neutrophil_pct, monocyte_pct, monocytes_abs, white_blood_cells, crp, pdw, hemoglobin, cortisol. Key absences: 'lym', 'wbc', 'wbc_total', 'plt', 'non_hdl', 'hgb', 'erythropoietin', 'jak2_v617f', 'rbc_count' are NOT in SSOT canonical keys.
changes.citation: Python: yaml.safe_load('backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml')['biomarkers'].keys() — full key list extracted

changes.result: CONFIRMED — CRITICAL ESTATE FINDING — 5 PRIMARY, 9 SUPPORTING NON-CANONICAL IDs
changes.finding: CONFIRMED. Python scan of all 41 PSI files against canonical SSOT keys. PRIMARY non-canonical (5 PSI files): (1) pkg_kb52d_non_hdl_cholesterol_high_atherogenic_lipoprotein_burden [p1_10] -> primary 'non_hdl' (SSOT: 'non_hdl_cholesterol'); (2) pkg_kb52c_plt_high_reactive_thrombocytosis [p1_11] -> primary 'plt' (SSOT: 'platelets'); (3) pkg_kb52c_plt_low_peripheral_consumption_or_immune_destruction [p1_11] -> primary 'plt' (SSOT: 'platelets'); (4) pkg_kb52c_lym_low_lymphopenia_stress_or_immunosuppression [p1_12] -> primary 'lym' (SSOT has 'lymphocytes' AND 'lymphocytes_abs' — identity ambiguous, medical decision required); (5) pkg_kb52c_wbc_high_reactive_leukocytosis [p1_12] -> primary 'wbc' (SSOT: 'white_blood_cells'). SUPPORTING non-canonical (9 instances): 'hgb' in urea_high [p1_10] (SSOT: 'hemoglobin'); 'erythropoietin' in hematocrit_high_absolute_erythrocytosis and hematocrit_high_relative_hemoconcentration [p1_11] (NOT in SSOT at all); 'jak2_v617f' in hematocrit_high_absolute_erythrocytosis [p1_11] (NOT in SSOT — genetic marker); 'rbc_count' in mcv_low_microcytosis_iron_deficiency [p1_11] (SSOT: 'rbc'); 'wbc' in plt_high and plt_low as supporting [p1_11]; 'wbc_total' in lym_low as supporting [p1_12]; 'lym' in wbc_high as supporting [p1_12]. The validator must report all of these — it must NOT decide canonical equivalents.
changes.citation: Python scan: all 41 PSI files in knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/ — biomarker IDs extracted and tested against backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml canonical key set; knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_12_batch_c/pkg_kb52c_lym_low_lymphopenia_stress_or_immunosuppression/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml:17 (primary: lym); knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_12_batch_c/pkg_kb52c_wbc_high_reactive_leukocytosis/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml:17 (primary: wbc)

changes.result: CONFIRMED — SUPPORTING MARKER ONLY, SSOT-CANONICAL BUT DERIVED
changes.finding: CONFIRMED. transferrin_saturation IS in SSOT canonical key list (confirmed by key extraction). It appears as a SUPPORTING marker only (never primary) in 7 PSI files: pkg_kb52c_ferritin_high_inflammatory_hyperferritinemia, pkg_kb52c_ferritin_high_iron_overload_context, pkg_kb52c_ferritin_low_iron_store_depletion, pkg_kb61_transferrin_high_iron_deficiency_transport_upregulation, pkg_kb52c_iron_high_hepatocellular_or_hemolytic_release, pkg_kb52c_iron_high_iron_overload_context, pkg_kb52c_iron_low_absolute_iron_deficiency [and iron_low_functional]. The sprint prompt identifies this as a BLOCKED_DERIVED_MARKER_DEPENDENCY carry-forward because transferrin_saturation is calculated from iron + transferrin (not directly measured as a standalone blood test in most labs). The validator should flag these 7 PSI files as having a derived-marker supporting dependency. This is NOT a primary-metric blocker for those PSIs, but is a supporting-marker dependency concern to report.
changes.citation: Python scan: transferrin_saturation found in 7 PSI files as supporting_marker; backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml (transferrin_saturation canonical key confirmed)

changes.finding: pkg_kb52c_lym_low_lymphopenia_stress_or_immunosuppression uses primary_metric.biomarker_id: 'lym'. 'lym' is NOT a canonical SSOT key. The SSOT has BOTH 'lymphocytes' and 'lymphocytes_abs' as separate canonical keys — the correct mapping is medically ambiguous (absolute lymphocyte count vs percentage-based vs relative). The sprint prompt explicitly states this is a carry-forward medical identity decision. Cursor must classify this PSI as BLOCKED_BIOMARKER_IDENTITY in the activation-readiness manifest and report. The validator must report 'lym is not a canonical SSOT biomarker key' but must NOT decide 'lym = lymphocytes_abs'. Do not attempt to resolve this in the sprint.
changes.citation: knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_12_batch_c/pkg_kb52c_lym_low_lymphopenia_stress_or_immunosuppression/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml:17 (biomarker_id: lym); Python SSOT key scan: 'lym' absent; SSOT has 'lymphocytes' and 'lymphocytes_abs' as separate keys; automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:§7 (lym identity decision carry-forward)

changes.finding: pkg_kb52c_wbc_high_reactive_leukocytosis uses primary_metric.biomarker_id: 'wbc'. 'wbc' is NOT a canonical SSOT key. The canonical SSOT key is 'white_blood_cells'. The validator must report this but must NOT decide 'wbc = white_blood_cells' — that is a future SSOT adjudication. This PSI must be classified BLOCKED_BIOMARKER_IDENTITY. Additionally, 'wbc_total' appears as a supporting marker in the lym_low PSI — also non-canonical, same SSOT key applies. Both must be reported without resolution.
changes.citation: knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_12_batch_c/pkg_kb52c_wbc_high_reactive_leukocytosis/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml:17 (biomarker_id: wbc); Python SSOT key scan: 'wbc' absent, 'white_blood_cells' is canonical; automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:§7 (wbc identity/SSOT decision carry-forward)

changes.finding: 'erythropoietin' appears as a supporting marker in pkg_kb52c_hematocrit_high_absolute_erythrocytosis and pkg_kb52c_hematocrit_high_relative_hemoconcentration [p1_11_batch_b]. 'jak2_v617f' appears as a supporting marker in pkg_kb52c_hematocrit_high_absolute_erythrocytosis [p1_11_batch_b]. Neither 'erythropoietin' nor 'jak2_v617f' is in the SSOT canonical key list — there is NO equivalent canonical key for either (not just a naming alias issue). These represent a qualitatively different category: markers for which no SSOT registration exists at all. The validator must report these as BLOCKED_BIOMARKER_IDENTITY at the supporting-marker level. The sprint must not decide whether to add these to SSOT — that is a separate SSOT authority decision.
changes.citation: Python SSOT key scan: 'erythropoietin' absent, 'jak2_v617f' absent (no canonical equivalent exists); knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_11_batch_b/pkg_kb52c_hematocrit_high_absolute_erythrocytosis/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml (not yet read directly — Cursor must confirm biomarker_ids during implementation)

changes.finding: 'plt' as primary metric in pkg_kb52c_plt_high_reactive_thrombocytosis and pkg_kb52c_plt_low_peripheral_consumption_or_immune_destruction [p1_11_batch_b] is not a canonical SSOT key. The canonical key is 'platelets'. Although 'plt' is a universally understood abbreviation, the validator must still report it as BLOCKED_BIOMARKER_IDENTITY and it must appear in the activation-readiness manifest as blocked. The identity decision (plt → platelets) is closer to a clerical correction than lym → lymphocytes/lymphocytes_abs, but it is still a future SSOT adjudication. Do not self-authorise this mapping in the sprint.
changes.citation: knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_11_batch_b/pkg_kb52c_plt_high_reactive_thrombocytosis/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml (primary: plt); Python SSOT key scan: 'plt' absent, 'platelets' is canonical

changes.finding: 'non_hdl' as primary metric in pkg_kb52d_non_hdl_cholesterol_high_atherogenic_lipoprotein_burden [p1_10_batch_a] is not a canonical SSOT key. The canonical key is 'non_hdl_cholesterol'. Similar to plt/platelets, this is a truncation alias. Validator must report BLOCKED_BIOMARKER_IDENTITY. Must appear in the activation-readiness manifest as blocked. Identity correction (non_hdl → non_hdl_cholesterol) is a future SSOT adjudication.
changes.citation: knowledge_bus/generated_pilot/p1_10_batch_a/pkg_kb52d_non_hdl_cholesterol_high_atherogenic_lipoprotein_burden/promoted_signal_intelligence.yaml (primary: non_hdl confirmed by Python scan); Python SSOT key scan: 'non_hdl' absent, 'non_hdl_cholesterol' is canonical

changes.finding: The sprint prompt draws a hard boundary at §Important boundary: the validator may report 'lym is not a canonical SSOT biomarker key' but must not decide 'lym = lymphocytes_abs'. This applies to ALL non-canonical IDs identified. The validator's job is to enumerate discrepancies; resolution is a future sprint with medical and SSOT authority. Cursor must not add any canonical-mapping logic to the validator.
changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:§Important boundary

changes.finding: Cursor must confirm at Stage 4A that no tooling files (.cursor/rules/, .vscode/, .codex/, AGENTS.md) are staged or committed to the sprint branch. SOP v1.3.1 non-negotiable: no tooling-file leakage into governed sprint branches. Precedent from P1-12 hardening.

overall_verdict: HARDENED. All 5 front-matter fields confirmed. All prerequisite docs (9 files) and staged PSI batch directories (3 directories, 41 PSI files, 41 compile manifests) confirmed present on main. SSOT canonical key list extracted from backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml and cross-referenced against entire PSI estate. Key findings for Cursor: (1) All 41 compile manifests have runtime_active: false — clean baseline; (2) 5 PRIMARY non-canonical biomarker IDs found across p1_10, p1_11, p1_12 batches (non_hdl, plt×2, lym, wbc); (3) 9 SUPPORTING non-canonical biomarker IDs found (hgb, erythropoietin×2, jak2_v617f, rbc_count, wbc×2, wbc_total, lym); (4) erythropoietin and jak2_v617f have NO SSOT equivalent at all; (5) transferrin_saturation IS canonical but appears as supporting marker in 7 PSI files — derived-marker dependency carry-forward; (6) hemoglobin PSI absent from estate — known source-support gap; (7) No existing validator checks SSOT canonicality — new validator is required and not duplicative. No STOP conditions triggered. Five mandatory hardening flags pre-load known estate issues for Cursor's audit and classification work. Cursor may proceed under TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH governance.
